context: Tests whether the agent correctly selects the transcription mode based on the input type: using --url for live stream URLs, batch file mode for local recordings, and --realtime for local file simulation. Also checks that transcribe.sh is used and quiet mode is applied in the automated context.

checklist.name: transcribe.sh entrypoint
checklist.description: The solution calls transcribe.sh as the underlying transcription tool rather than reimplementing transcription logic directly

checklist.name: URL mode for streams
checklist.description: The transcribe.sh invocation uses --url for handling live stream URLs

checklist.name: Batch mode for local files
checklist.description: The transcribe.sh invocation passes the file path as a positional argument (without --url) for local audio files

checklist.name: Quiet flag in automation
checklist.description: The transcribe.sh invocation includes -q or --quiet to suppress status messages

checklist.name: Distinct input type branches
checklist.description: The script contains distinct code paths or branches for URL inputs versus local file inputs (e.g., if/else or case statement checking whether the argument starts with http:// or https://)

checklist.name: Realtime flag for file simulation
checklist.description: The script includes or documents use of --realtime for streaming a local file in realtime mode (e.g., for testing or simulating a live feed from an archive file)
